column1: # type is one of: backend, infra, frontend, testing, docs

column3: Set up repo, Spring Boot 3 + Java 17 skeleton
column4: Initialize Spring Boot project (Java 17), set up Docker Compose with Postgres, configure application.yml profiles (local/test).
Configure Flyway, write V1__init.sql creating the schema baseline.

column3: Create User and Application entities + repositories
column4: Define JPA entities, Spring Data repositories, basic mapping (no auth yet).
Controller + service for create/read/update/delete on Application, no security yet.
Register/login endpoints, password hashing, JWT issuing and validation filter.
Secure existing endpoints by role, add @PreAuthorize where relevant.
Set up Testcontainers Postgres module, write integration test for ApplicationRepository.
Define relationships: Application 1-to-many Interview, many-to-many Tag, 1-to-many ResumeVersion.
Service enforcing legal status transitions (APPLIED -> INTERVIEWING -> OFFER/REJECTED), domain exception on illegal transition.
@ControllerAdvice mapping domain exceptions to proper HTTP status + error body.

column3: Unit tests for state machine service
column4: Mockito-based unit tests covering legal and illegal transitions.

column3: Add pagination/sorting/filtering to Application list endpoint
column4: Use Pageable + Specifications for filtering by status, date range, tag.
Handwritten JPQL/native query for counts by status and response rate; inspect generated SQL for N+1 awareness.
Configure springdoc-openapi, annotate key endpoints.
Configure MinIO container, create bucket, wire S3 SDK client config in Spring.
ResumeVersion upload to MinIO, download/list endpoints, versioning per application.
If feasible, use MinIO Testcontainers module to test upload/download round-trip.

column3: Add RabbitMQ to docker-compose + Spring AMQP config
column4: Set up exchange/queue/binding config, connection in application.yml.
REST endpoint accepting pasted email body, publishes event message to RabbitMQ.
Basic consumer that logs and acks messages, no parsing logic yet.

column3: Build email parsing chain (Chain of Responsibility)
column4: EmailParseStep interface, ordered chain: RejectionKeywordStep, InterviewInviteStep, fallback step.

column3: Wire consumer to apply parsed result
column4: Consumer updates Application status based on parse chain result.

column3: Add dead-letter queue for unparseable messages
column4: Configure DLQ, route failed/unmatched messages there.
RabbitMQ Testcontainers module, test publish -> consume -> status update flow.
@Scheduled job scanning upcoming interviews, writes to Notification table.
Standalone components, login/register pages, JWT HTTP interceptor, routing skeleton.
Table or kanban board, wired to backend pagination/filtering.
Shows interviews, resume versions, tags for a single application.
Upload form, version list display.
Surface scheduled reminders in the UI.
Backend test job (mvn/gradle test), Angular build/lint job, run on push.
Review coverage report, add missing unit/integration tests identified during the build.
Set up Railway or Fly.io project, configure environment variables/secrets.

column3: Deploy full stack (app, Postgres, RabbitMQ, MinIO)
column4: Get all services live, resolve networking/config issues, verify end-to-end.
Visual diagram of services, data flow, async pipeline.
Local docker-compose setup, env vars, how to run tests, link to live deploy.
Document key choices (state machine vs enum, RabbitMQ vs @Async, Specifications vs QueryDSL, etc.) with reasoning.
Write out answers to likely questions: challenging feature, what you'd change at scale, testing strategy.
